rationale: The citing paper uses Chandy et al. (2001) as a key prior study on informational versus emotional TV ad appeals and explicitly discusses its main moderator finding (market age) in the literature review. It positions its own contribution against that work by arguing that prior studies, including Chandy et al., did not examine how appeal effectiveness varies by product positioning and did not jointly model online search and sales. The paper also references Chandy et al. for measurement practice, noting similar operationalization of informational and emotional content as counts of coded cues.
relation_description: The citing study extends Chandy et al. (2001) by examining the same broad appeal distinction (informational vs emotional) in a new setting with larger multi-product data, adding product price/quality as moderators, and modeling both online search and sales outcomes together. It builds on Chandy et al.'s empirical ad-coding tradition while broadening the boundary conditions and outcomes studied.
